images.caption: Figure 1:Example of the catastrophic forgetting problem in tactile alignment for MLLMs (e.g., TVL[21]w/ Qwen2.5-VL-3B). A small amount of tactile training set often forgets the visual sense in the base MLLM. More failure cases in Appendix.

Figure 2:The overall framework ofSplash. To mitigate the catastrophic forgetting in the visual aspect, we train a dormant subspace in LLM for tactile alignment in sMLLMs. We note that the tactile front-end is also updated in a unified training stage.

Figure 3:Quantitative comparison with objective metrics on SSVTP[27], TVL[21], and TacQuad[16]. We report the averaged F1 score and Top-5 accuracy.Splashdemonstrates superior performance in both F1 and Top-5 Accuracy.

Figure 5:Qualitative comparisons on TacQuad[16].
BothSplash-1B and -3B generate more diverse predictions than baselines, achieving higher accuracy scores by an LLM judge.
Figure 0.A1:More examples of the catastrophic forgetting problem in tactile alignment for MLLMs.

Figure 0.A2:OWL-inspired adaptive sparsity allocation under a global 60% sparsity budget. Green bars denote the Layer-wise Outlier Distribution (LOD), while the orange curve represents the resulting adaptive sparsity ratio assigned to each transformer layer.

Figure 0.A3:Decomposed parameter shifts in the trainable dormant subspace.
The plots show theΔ​log10\Delta\log_{10}distributions (Splashminus baseline) for (Left) weight magnitude|𝐖i,j||\mathbf{W}_{i,j}|, (Middle) activationℓ2\ell_{2}-norm‖𝐱j‖2\|\mathbf{x}_{j}\|_{2}, and (Right) overall importance score𝐒i,j\mathbf{S}_{i,j}.
The prominent rightward shift in activation confirms its primary role in accommodating the newly introduced tactile modality.

Figure 0.A4:Qualitative failure cases in visuo-tactile perception.
(Top) Material misclassification on SSVTP, where a soft fabric surface is incorrectly predicted as a rigid metallic material.
(Bottom) Micro-texture misinterpretation on TVL, where a bumpy matte surface is predicted as smooth and glossy.

Figure 0.A5:Additional qualitative comparisons on TacQuad.
Each example displays the natural RGB imageXRX_{R}, the tactile contact imageXCX_{C}, and the predicted texts from the baselines and our models alongside their evaluation scores (out of 10).
